title: Heartbeat to detect dead connections
description: Ping/pong or keepalive with a timeout reaps half-open connections so resources are not leaked by silent drops.
body: Use when connections can drop without a clean close (mobile networks, NAT timeouts, crashed clients). Do not rely on TCP alone to notice a dead peer, which can leave half-open connections for minutes. Actions: send periodic pings (or SSE comments), expect a pong/activity within a timeout, and close and clean up connections that miss it; on the client, treat missed heartbeats as a trigger to reconnect. Validation signals include a test that simulates a silent drop and asserts the server closes and frees the connection within the timeout. Risk boundary: no heartbeat lets dead connections accumulate until file-descriptor or memory limits are hit, causing a slow outage.
